children.name: Query — list users
children.notes: Plain query with inline enum (role) + boolean (active) arguments; returns a list of User objects. Exercises the GraphQL body editor and a successful data response.

children.name: Query with variables — get user
children.notes: Named operation with a typed ($id: ID!) variable supplied in the Variables pane. Verifies variable substitution into the query.

children.name: Mutation — create user
children.notes: Mutation operation with variables. Confirms the editor sends mutations and round-trips the created object.

children.name: Introspection — fetch schema
children.notes: Introspection query (__schema). The mock returns its canned schema — the same data the editor's 'Fetch schema' button uses to power autocomplete/validation.

children.name: Error — unknown field
children.notes: Queries a field that does not exist. The mock replies HTTP 200 with a GraphQL `errors` array (GraphQL convention) so the editor's error rendering can be exercised.

children.name: Echo (text)
children.notes: Connect, then Send the text message — the server echoes every frame straight back. Basic text round-trip.

children.name: Echo (JSON)
children.notes: Same echo endpoint with the composer in JSON mode — verifies JSON-formatted outbound messages are sent and echoed.

children.name: Time feed (server push)
children.notes: Server-initiated frames: pushes a timestamped JSON frame once per second with no client message required. Exercises received-without-send traffic.

children.name: Subprotocols
children.notes: Offers Sec-WebSocket-Protocol values in the handshake; the server accepts the first (chat). Confirms subprotocol negotiation appears in the console.

children.name: Reject (handshake failure)
children.notes: The server refuses the upgrade with HTTP 401. Verifies the console surfaces a failed handshake as an error rather than an open socket.

children.name: Events (named + id)
children.notes: Structured Server-Sent Events: each frame carries `event: tick`, an incrementing `id:` and a JSON `data:` payload. text/event-stream auto-streams live on Send. Tune count/interval in the query string.

children.name: Counter (bare data)
children.notes: Untagged default ('message') events whose data is just a bare number — exercises the viewer's no-event-name path.

children.name: LLM token stream
children.notes: OpenAI-style chat.completion.chunk deltas, one token at a time, terminated by `data: [DONE]`. Demonstrates incremental token rendering.

children.name: Infinite stream (use Stop)
children.notes: Never-ending tick stream with `:` keep-alive comments. Use the Stop button to end it; also tests the bounded in-memory log and save-while-running.

children.name: NDJSON (needs Stream toggle)
children.notes: Newline-delimited JSON (application/x-ndjson). This does NOT auto-stream — enable Settings → Request → 'Stream NDJSON responses live' first, otherwise it buffers and returns whole.

children.name: Stream index (not a stream)
children.notes: Control case: a plain JSON index describing the streaming endpoints. Returns buffered (no text/event-stream), confirming non-stream responses are unaffected.
